mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of co-occurring positive growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production during an early expansion phase reflects strengthening factory output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant (t-stat < 1.5) when applied outside the early expansion regime, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year consumer price index growth rate exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of threshold-crossing instances, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll growth (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and month-over-month increases in retail sales validate a synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of synchronized growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal an imminent supply-side contraction or recessionary shock, which forces equity markets to price in lower future cash flows and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with a Rank IC above 0.04 during historical recession onsets.
